title: edgesForLens + walkDescendants exposed as SDK functions but not wrapped as registered pi tools
body:
Forcing context: both functions (packages/pi-context/src/project-context.ts) are pure graph operations over Edge[] — edgesForLens(lens, items, authoredEdges) returns the materialized edge set for a lens (synthetic or hand-curated); walkDescendants(parentId, relationType, edges) returns the closure-table descendants under a relation_type. SDK functions exist with full test coverage. No pi.registerTool wrappers — LLMs cannot inspect lens edges or walk hierarchies via tool-use today.

Proposed shape: register two tools — 'project-edges-for-lens' (params: lensId; computes via getProjectContext + edgesForLens; returns Edge[]) and 'project-walk-descendants' (params: parentId, relationType; computes via getProjectContext + walkDescendants; returns string[] of descendant ids). Both tools enable LLM-driven exploration of the substrate hierarchy — required for any agent workflow that wants to navigate closure-table relations programmatically.

Combined into one issue per the user's gap inventory because both are pure-graph traversal functions sharing implementation pattern (resolve config + pull edges + run pure function).
resolved_by: Resolved in commit ad03a00 — feat(pi-project): substrate consumption surface. /project view + /project lens-curate subcommands plus project-validate-relations + project-edges-for-lens + project-walk-descendants pi tools registered, all built on a new lens-view.ts pure-function module (loadLensView, renderLensView, buildCurationSuggestions, validateProjectRelations, edgesForLensByName, walkLensDescendants). 17 new tests; pi-project workspace at 256/256.
